Hi Katie,

Thanks for checking in again.

Willow's arrival and departure times have been much more consistent, which has been great!  She is such a polite and lovely young woman, and beautiful with our children.

I will be honest, the feedback from my team is that they are still not feeling a huge amount of 'value add' for our children's learning and development (on top of what they already do each and every day). Whether this program ends up being the right fit for what we are looking for right at this moment or not, I really appreciate the effort you have gone to in seeking our feedback and trying to improve our experience.

Thanks so much,

Kindest Regards,

Hi  Monique,

I am very sorry to hear your feedback, as we pride ourselves on the high quality of our programs and their alignment with the ELF.

I appreciate you providing such comprehensive details. To ensure your next session meets your expectations, I will send one of my leaders, Coach Aisa to attend the session with Fanta. They will provide support and ensure the program is being delivered to our required standards, specifically regarding the level of engagement and "upskilling" for the older children and ensure it is meeting your needs.
If there are specific skills you expected the children to learn in these first four weeks, please share them with me. We can then review where those skills fit into the 46 week program and shuffling some more advanced skills forward provided the children are demonstrating the required safety and foundational skills for progression.
I look forward to hearing about the next session .
I have attached Aisa's WWC Blue Card below.
 

Kind Regards,

Katie

GC&E Rochedale South
Jun 18, 2026, 3:05 PM (1 day ago)
to me

Hi Katie,

 

Thanks for your email, and I’m pretty sure that I gave an emailed feedback the last time.

 

Offhand, I’m not impressed that the teacher was late again – arrived 9:40am. While I understand that last week she was still looking for the Centre, but today nothing has changed. We try to get the children organised to be ready for the activity before 9:30am, but this does not seem to matter as your teacher is late.

 

I was also expecting an improvement in the activity, and because you said that she will need to build on the skills of the children. The children in Kindergarten 1 did the same activities as with last week, which were much suited for smaller children. These activities were too easy for the older children. I didn’t see any “upskilling” in terms of what the children could do or explore.

 

It is unlikely that we will continue with the program if we do not see an improvement in the content and/or delivery.  I hope there is more to this gymnastics program apart from balancing bean bags on children’s heads.

 

Thanks again for your support.

 

 

Kind regards,

 

Monique Orendain

Centre Manager

patterns.prevention:
• Assign one consistent primary coach per centre for the first 8 weeks, with a named backup only if the primary is unavailable.

• Confirm coach availability 48 hours before each session and notify centres immediately of any changes.

• Contact centres within 24 hours of any missed or rescheduled session to confirm next session date and coach name.

• Review coach allocation weekly and flag centres with more than one coach change in their first month for manager intervention.

patterns.prevention:
• Audit all current coaches for engagement and energy levels; identify and address underperformers within 2 weeks.

• Establish a backup coach system so no centre operates without a replacement plan when their primary coach leaves or underperforms.

• Schedule pre-cancellation check-ins with centres at the 4-session mark to catch dissatisfaction early and course-correct.

• Review and upgrade program content and equipment standards to ensure age-appropriate challenge levels that keep children engaged.

patterns.prevention:
• Contact centres currently scheduled for term 3+ to confirm commitment and identify any alternative providers being considered.

• Schedule check-in calls with all active centres at 6-week and 12-week milestones to address scheduling conflicts or program fit issues before cancellation requests occur.

• Review pricing and contract terms against competitor incursion providers to assess competitiveness, particularly around flexible scheduling and customisation options.

• Establish a "retention conversation" protocol when centres mention budget constraints or scheduling issues—offer solutions (time slots, bundled rates, internal hybrid models) before they switch.

Summary of their time with us: 
The first week of sessions was described as going well, with the coach, Mateu  noted for being polite, professional, and on time, and there were no concerns raised at this early stage. As the program moved into December 2025, feedback remained stable and positive, with no additional bookings requested at that point but still no issues identified, reinforcing that the program had been well received from the outset.

In January 2026, minor operational adjustments were made to better suit the centre’s schedule, including trialling session times between 1:00 pm and 2:30 pm, which were later confirmed as workable. This period reflected a practical alignment phase where the service was being fine-tuned to fit the centre’s routine while maintaining strong satisfaction. By February 2026, direct feedback from the centre continued to be highly positive, with Mateus again described as “great” and “amazing,” consistently on time, and with no complaints raised, indicating growing confidence in the program’s reliability.

In March 2026, further internal coordination took place, including a confirmed adjustment to a 2:00 pm start time, showing continued responsiveness to centre needs. Around the same time, broader feedback highlighted very strong satisfaction, with both coaches Mateus Gomes and Patricia (Patsy) Del Valle being praised for their energy, engagement, and ability to connect with children. Staff noted that the children were actively enjoying the sessions, and the coaches were described as having positive, bubbly personalities and being highly attentive and professional.

By April 2026, the program had fully stabilised and was operating at a consistently high level, with explicit feedback confirming that Mateus was engaging well with children and delivering sessions that were both enjoyable and well-received. Staff reiterated that children loved the activities and that the coach was a strong positive influence in the program, with no concerns raised at this stage.

In May 2026, feedback continued to reflect satisfaction with the program, with the centre confirming they were happy with the sessions and the coach, although Aletia was noted as being away at the time. Shortly after, the centre made the decision to cancel the program due to budget constraints rather than any issues with delivery or satisfaction. The final communication acknowledged that the service had been well liked and that both the coach and sessions had been appreciated, with an indication that they may reconnect in future when budget allows.

Overall, the timeline reflects a consistently positive service experience from start to finish, characterised by strong coach performance, high child engagement, smooth operational adjustments, and stable satisfaction, with the only reason for discontinuation being external financial constraints rather than program quality or delivery issues.

Next point of contact: purely budget happy to have us back asap when they have budget

The Mini Monstars program journey began on 22 May 2025 with an initial welcome, followed by the introduction of Coach Azaria on 28 May 2025. While the first session on 2 June 2025 presented initial engagement challenges, a follow-up on 17 June 2025 showed significant improvement that was well-received by the educators.

Throughout the second half of 2025, the Kindy room maintained high engagement under the leadership of a new Director, Em. Key developments included:

  - August 2025: Transitioned to Coach Janai, who received rave reviews for high energy through September.
  - October 2025: Program adapted to indoor gymnastics to accommodate the heat; center staff (Tegan) noted positive feedback regarding the format and coaching staff.
  - December 2025: The year concluded on 16 December 2025.

The program resumed in January 2026 with a new schedule of Wednesdays at 9:30 AM. Coaching transitions continued as follows:

  - March 2026: Following Brendan's resignation, Coach Emily became the lead on 18 March 2026.
  - April 2026: Feedback on 2 April confirmed satisfaction with Emily’s performance, despite the loss of the male connection previously provided by Brendan.

The partnership reached its conclusion following a formal notice of cancellation received on 5 May 2026, cited as a result of low occupancy and changing circumstances. In the final weeks, focus shifted to supporting the center's occupancy growth, including a strategic discussion on 20 May 2026. strong feedback regarding the value of the program and Coach Charlotte's development over time. However, there were several opportunities where we could have strengthened the relationship further. Early in the partnership, feedback received on 20 January 2026 indicated that sessions were initially "rocky," with concerns around coach engagement, one-on-one interaction with children, and communication with educators. While subsequent feedback in February and March showed significant improvement and increasing satisfaction with Charlotte, earlier intervention, coaching support, and follow-up observations may have accelerated this progress and strengthened confidence sooner. Despite these areas for improvement, the centre consistently expressed that they loved the sessions and that the eventual cancellation was solely the result of a head office decision to remove incursion budgets across centres rather than dissatisfaction with Mini Monstars. The positive relationship was maintained throughout the cancellation process, and the centre specifically invited future discussions in the new financial year. Going forward, earlier coach support, stronger educator-level engagement, streamlined invoicing assistance, and more consistent communication pathways could further strengthen similar centre relationships and improve retention opportunities where external budget pressures arise.

centres.name: Papilio Early Learning Robina
centres.closedDesc:
Reason for leaving affinity purely budget
summary of time with us
Dec 2025 2025, with initial feedback during November and December indicating that children were engaged and enjoying the sessions. Some early developmental feedback was provided for Coach Mateus, including increasing engagement and energy levels with older children, improving time management across Kindy groups, and creating clearer progression between activities. Following the Christmas period, sessions resumed in January 2026, with final schedule details confirmed on 20 January. On 28 January 2026, more detailed feedback was received from the centre regarding Coach Mateus, with educators noting that while he was well-liked and engaged positively with children, sessions appeared repetitive, lacked visible progression between age groups, and required stronger structure and preparation. Mini Monstars responded immediately by implementing a support plan, arranging for Coach Patsy to shadow sessions and preparing for a coaching transition. However, Patsy's unexpected unavailability resulted in further disruption, and on 11 February 2026 the centre raised concerns after Mateus attended alone when a transition had been expected. Mini Monstars acted quickly and permanently reassigned the service to Coach Aisa, communicating the change and providing reassurance to the centre. The impact was immediate, with feedback on 24 February 2026 describing Aisa as "amazing, incredible and perfect," and on 20 April 2026 Melanie confirmed that the children loved the sessions, had no complaints, and that Aisa was doing a fantastic job. During this period, Mini Monstars also worked closely with the centre regarding Affinity invoicing processes and payment procedures, helping navigate several administrative challenges between February and April. On 30 April 2026, after the centre advised they were experiencing budget pressures, Mini Monstars proposed a significantly reduced session structure and discounted pricing model in an attempt to retain services while reducing costs. Despite these efforts, Melanie advised on 12 and 13 May 2026 that the centre was unable to continue due to budget limitations. She reiterated that the decision was not related to program quality, expressed how much the centre loved Coach Aisa and the sessions, and advised they would like Mini Monstars to return as soon as budgets allowed in the new financial year. Following cancellation, Mini Monstars continued supporting the centre with invoice clarification requests throughout June 2026. Overall, Papilio Early Learning Robina's journey included an initially challenging coaching period that was successfully resolved through decisive action and a coach change, resulting in a highly positive experience under Coach Aisa. The centre's eventual cancellation was driven by budget constraints rather than dissatisfaction with the program, and the relationship concluded with a strong opportunity for future re-engagement

Summary of their time with us: 

The center originally had Katie as their coach and thoroughly enjoyed the sessions. However, after being transferred to Elaina at the Wooloowin location, they were unhappy and submitted a cancellation request. To retain the center, we brought in a high-level coach, Brendan, to lead the music sessions. This was successful and the center retracted their cancellation; however, Brendan suddenly resigned after only five sessions.

Following Brendan's departure, one session was missed due to a lack of available coaches. We subsequently placed Chi in the role. While Chi's performance was acceptable, they were not the high-level coach required to keep this specific center satisfied.

I followed up several times for feedback but was initially told that the director, Liz, was too busy to speak. When I eventually reached Liz, she noted that Chi needed to use more instruments and fewer MP3s. In a later follow-up, Liz claimed that Chi had implemented this feedback and improved. However, Chi advised me that she had not actually run a session since that feedback was given, leaving the validity of the center's comments in question.

Ultimately, I believe that if we had a higher-level coach available to replace Brendan immediately when he quit, the center would likely still be running sessions with us.

came on 8th Oct 25 Didn't return in new year or cancel had to follow up and eventually Rhi got con tact for last 4 sessions however sessions set 1-4pm and they cancelled many of the last session as they said time didn't suit but also wouldn't change time, Noah also was a new coach and we stuggle to fiund coaches at that stage

  - Oct 8–10: Service commenced with positive feedback for older children.
  - Oct 16–24: Initial time changes were requested to avoid lunch clashes; however, feedback from the center contact, Liz, was limited as she did not work on session days.
  - Nov – Dec: Standard check-ins occurred. The center confirmed they were happy with the sessions, and the final date for 2025 was set for December 18th.

January – February 2026: Cancellation and Notice Period

  - Jan 19: Liz emailed to cancel the service due to head office budget cuts. I confirmed the four-week notice period, setting the final week for February 19th.
  - Feb 13–16: Efforts were made to save the account by offering different session types (music). I spoke with Sophie Jones to provide the formal four-session notice information.

March 2026: Scheduling Conflicts and Billing Disputes

  - Mar 5–9: Coach Noah reported significant issues; the center was unaware of his arrival, and the 1:00 PM – 4:00 PM slot clashed with nap times. I suggested moving the remaining sessions to a morning slot (8:30 AM – 11:30 AM).
  - Mar 12–18: Sophie Jones (Senior Manager) stated they could not accommodate the new times due to other bookings. She questioned if the final four weeks had been paid for or required an invoice.
  - Mar 23: The center cancelled the final session with Noah, citing the unsuitable time and their existing bookings.

Next point of contact:
Dollow up term 4
